title: Cirrus: Disable most periodic services/timers
body:
Depends on #3534

For CI testing, it's important to remove as much variability from the
overall system as possible.  This permits focusing just on problems
closely related to code-changes.  To this end, and because VMs are very
short-lived (2 hours at most), disable all systemd services and timers
which perform periodic activities.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: ***NOTE:*** This is speculative / preventative change.  By chance I noticed an apt-system-update process running in an Ubuntu VM during testing.   I'm not aware of any evidence this or other services causing testing flakes, though it's certainly possible.  Best we just completely avoid the possibility.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago Okay, simplified this greatly. Now it just checks if any of the evil systemd units are active.  I'm pretty sure it was the `apt-daily-upgrade` and `apt-daily` that were the main cause.  Both could/would run for minutes after VM boots, especially if the package repos were slow at the time.   Seem good 'nuff?
Once again for the eleventh-time, and umpteen billion typo and goof fixes (thanks Ed).
one nit, LGTM but tests aren't happy.  I don't think but don't know that they're not related.

Okay, Tom's nit is dead and I rebased the code.

@edsantiago just for FYI: See [the warning here about slash-lgtm](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#bot-interactions).  That section exists because I accidentally caused one of Matt's not-yet-ready major changes to merge, right before release, on a Friday, and totally FUBAR the ship up.  We have some safeguards in place, but they're fragile.  An actual fix (getting google k8s prow <-> GitHub-Actions chatting) is very much a SMOP.  Just something to be mindful of.
LGTM. (My earlier slash was deliberate; I was not aware of any pending review)

@edsantiago The /hold was to prevent tests from spinning...

The mergebot is unaware of the status Cirrus is reporting, so if the Prow tests are green and the LGTM label is present, it will spin trying to merge while Github tells it there are failing tests. Our current manual solution is to /hold until tests go green.

>it will spin trying to merge while Github tells it there are failing tests

Thanks - that wasn't clear from the CONTRIBUTING guide. I get it now.
